findings.summary: CLAUDE.md code structure map lists multiple classes at wrong paths or that no longer exist
evidence: CLAUDE.md line 111 lists `api/GrammarConfigSerializer.java` — this class exists only in `src/test/`, not in production sources
CLAUDE.md line 124 lists `exploration/ExplorationService.java` — the actual file is at `exploration/spi/ExplorationService.java`
CLAUDE.md line 133 lists `model/FetchExamplesOptions.java` — the actual file is at `model/exploration/FetchExamplesOptions.java`
CLAUDE.md line 135 lists `model/QueryResults.java` — this file does not exist; result types live under `model/sketch/`
CLAUDE.md line 136 lists `model/RelationType.java` — the actual file is at `config/RelationType.java`
CLAUDE.md lines 157-159 list `viz/Edge.java` and `viz/RelationEdgeType.java` — both actually live under `model/exploration/`
CLAUDE.md line 120 lists `config/RelationPatternBuilder.java` — this file does not exist
CLAUDE.md omits `api/ComparisonResponseAssembler.java`, `api/SketchResponseAssembler.java`, and the entire `query/spi/` package

An older spec section within CLAUDE.md (visible in the embedded concept-sketch-spec content) still references removed packages `grammar/` and `tagging/` and non-existent classes CQLParser, CQLPattern, CQLToLuceneCompiler, WordSketchQueryExecutor, SimpleTagger, LongIntHashMap, CQLVerifier — none of which exist in the current codebase
CLAUDE.md performance-tuning section references `CollocationsBuilderV2` and `HybridLuceneIndexer` — neither exists
findings.suggestion: Regenerate the `## Code Structure` section of CLAUDE.md by walking `src/main/java` and updating all class paths, removing non-existent entries, and adding the missing classes. Remove or clearly archive the stale spec content that references the old `grammar/` and `tagging/` packages.

findings.summary: Two endpoints serve concordance examples under different URL prefixes with diverging error semantics
evidence: GET /api/semantic-field/examples returns 503 ExplorationException if the corpus fails; GET /api/concordance/examples returns 500 IOException for the same failure

GET /api/concordance/examples silently falls back to a proximity pattern when the relation is unknown (returns fallback:true in body); GET /api/semantic-field/examples throws 400 for the same condition — a caller switching between the two endpoints gets different behavior for the same invalid relation

Both endpoints accept identical parameter names (seed, collocate, relation, top) and return the same ExamplesResponse record, making the URL-level split invisible to consumers who must also memorise the diverging error contracts
findings.suggestion: Document the intended difference explicitly at the HTTP level — either via distinct error codes (e.g., 422 for unknown relation vs 503 for corpus failure) or by consolidating the two endpoints behind a single path with a `mode=semantic|concordance` parameter. If the fallback behaviour in the concordance endpoint is intentional, add a clear note in both Javadocs.

findings.summary: The `dep` keyword is overloaded as both a sketch-type discriminator and a URL path segment in /api/sketch/
evidence: routeSketchRequest splits the path and checks if parts[1].equals(RelationType.DEP.label()) — meaning a lemma named exactly `dep` would be misrouted to the dependency sketch handler instead of the single-relation surface handler
The server Javadoc lists both `/api/sketch/{lemma}/dep` and `/api/sketch/{lemma}/{relation}` but does not document the collision risk or that `dep` is a reserved segment
The relation ID namespace and the `dep` sketch-type keyword share the same URL position without a type prefix (e.g., `/api/sketch/{lemma}/type/dep` or a query param)
findings.suggestion: Reserve the `dep` path segment explicitly in Javadoc and validate that no grammar relation can have id=`dep`. Alternatively, move the sketch-type discriminator to a query parameter (`?type=dep`) to eliminate the ambiguity entirely.

findings.summary: PosGroup (domain enum) lives in model/ while the analogous RelationType enum lives in config/
evidence: PosGroup is a domain enum (NOUN, VERB, ADJ, ADV, OTHER) used by SemanticFieldExplorer and RelationUtils — it lives in the model/ root package
RelationType (SURFACE, DEP) is also a domain enum used by SketchHandlers and GrammarConfig — it lives in config/
Both enums are used across the config/, exploration/, and api/ layers but are homed in different packages with no documented rationale for the asymmetry
findings.suggestion: Move both enums to a single location — either both in config/ (since they model grammar-level distinctions) or both in model/ (since they represent domain classifications). Document the chosen convention in the model/ or config/ package-info.
